stadiums.name: Wankhede Stadium, Mumbai
stadiums.characteristics: High bounce, assists pace initially but becomes excellent for batting. Dew is a major factor in the second innings.
stadiums.short_boundary_dimensions: Square boundaries are short (approx 64-68m), straight boundaries are around 72m.

stadiums.name: M. Chinnaswamy Stadium, Bengaluru
stadiums.characteristics: Extremely flat track, very little assistance for bowlers. High altitude means the ball travels further.
stadiums.short_boundary_dimensions: Very short all around, particularly square (approx 60-65m).

stadiums.name: MA Chidambaram Stadium (Chepauk), Chennai
stadiums.characteristics: Traditionally slow and low, offering significant turn. Spinners play a crucial role. Batting gets tougher as the game progresses.
stadiums.short_boundary_dimensions: Symmetrical, decent size. Square boundaries around 68-70m.

stadiums.name: Eden Gardens, Kolkata
stadiums.characteristics: Good batting surface, but offers early swing for pacers. Spinners get purchase under lights. Fast outfield.

stadiums.name: Narendra Modi Stadium, Ahmedabad
stadiums.characteristics: Multiple pitches. Red soil offers bounce and pace, black soil offers turn. Generally a good batting surface.
stadiums.short_boundary_dimensions: Very large ground. Straight boundaries are 75m+, square around 70m.
